title: Composite Cell Value-Stream Redesign
description: Map the workflow of a composite subassembly cell, identify waste, and propose a revised flow that improves cycle time and first-pass yield.
whyItMatters: Aerospace competitiveness is not only about elegant design. Manufacturing flow, rework, and traceability strongly influence whether a programme can deliver aircraft on time and at sustainable cost.

steps.title: Choose the cell and product
steps.description: Define the composite part, the current process steps, and the teams involved from layup through inspection.
steps.tips: A narrow scope produces a more believable redesign.

steps.title: Map the current state
steps.description: Record process time, waiting time, queues, rework loops, and approval handoffs for each step.
steps.tips: Waiting and rework often dominate the real cycle time.

steps.title: Identify the biggest waste drivers
steps.description: Mark where defects are discovered late, tools are unavailable, or information arrives too slowly.
steps.tips: Do not treat every annoyance as equally important.

steps.title: Design a future-state flow
steps.description: Propose layout, sequencing, standard work, or inspection changes that improve flow without weakening traceability.
steps.tips: In aerospace, speed without quality discipline is not improvement.

steps.title: Estimate the operational impact
steps.description: Quantify likely gains in cycle time, queue reduction, or first-pass yield and call out the main implementation risks.
steps.tips: A rough but reasoned estimate is better than a vague promise.

rubric.description: The current state reflects realistic flow, approvals, and quality constraints.
The redesign addresses root causes instead of cosmetic changes.
The proposal respects traceability and quality requirements.
Expected gains and risks are explained credibly.
